summary: Backend pytest suite executed against external ingress (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). 14/16 pass, 1 xfail (Luma multi-turn — EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget cap), 1 hard fail (Stripe checkout status 500). All auth/role/portfolio/services/bookings/documents/invoices/galleries/Luma-first-turn flows pass.

critical.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error immediately after a successful POST /api/payments/checkout/invoice. The Emergent Stripe proxy returns 404 ('No such checkout.session') when retrieving the session that was just minted ~250ms earlier; the route does not catch emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError and propagates a bare 500 to the client. Reproduced 4 times in a row with delays of 0/2/5/10s — looks like Emergent Stripe test infra returns the wrong session_id namespace or is eventually-consistent and the lookup never lands. Either way the route must wrap the call in try/except and surface a 502/404 with detail so the success-redirect page doesn't crash.

Second+ turns intermittently return 500 because litellm raises BadRequestError 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.001164, Max budget: 0.001' from the Emergent LLM proxy. Root cause is the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget cap on this test environment ($0.001), but luma_routes.chat does not wrap chat_step in try/except — any litellm exception becomes a raw 500. Add error handling so the API returns a graceful 502 with a friendly reply (e.g. handoff-to-human) when the LLM provider is unavailable. Code logic itself is correct — first turn succeeded and called get_active_services exactly as specified.

minor.issue: Stub returns HTTP 501 'Use /login'. Spec lists it as supported. Not used by current frontend tests but should either be implemented or removed from public docs.
Silently lowercases the submitted email but does not document this; response echoes the lowercased form. Cosmetic — adjust docs or echo verbatim.

action_items: Wrap `await stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` in /app/backend/routes/payments_routes.py inside try/except CheckoutError; return HTTPException(502, ...) or a graceful pending response. Also investigate why the Emergent Stripe proxy returns 404 for a session it just created — may be an infra issue worth flagging to the Emergent payments team.

Wrap `await chat_step(...)` in /app/backend/routes/luma_routes.py with try/except for litellm exceptions (BadRequestError/RateLimitError/etc.) and return a friendly fallback reply with status 200 or 503. Note: current budget cap on EMERGENT_LLM_KEY ($0.001) is so low that tool-calling barely lasts one turn — request a higher cap if available.
Decide if /api/auth/register should preserve original email casing or document the lowercase normalisation.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py cookies use samesite='none' + secure=True — correct for cross-site cookie auth on the preview ingress (HTTPS); good.
All MongoDB reads consistently project out _id — clean.
luma/agent.py: parallel_tool_calls=False is set explicitly so tools execute serially; that simplifies state. Good.
payments_routes.py status endpoint has zero error handling around the third-party SDK call — fragile (see HIGH issue above).
luma_routes.chat has zero error handling around chat_step — fragile (see HIGH issue above).
bookings_routes.change_status accepts `status` as a query param via FastAPI — works, but consider a Pydantic body for consistency.
galleries_routes /photo/{blob_id} requires auth but does not verify ownership; documented inline but worth a stricter check if photos are sensitive.
seed.py + on_startup run on every restart; the seed must be idempotent (admin update if password changed) — please confirm; current tests show admin login working post-restart.
documents/sign requires non-empty signature_name and rejects re-sign with 400 — good.
test_credentials: Admin photographer@illuminatestudios.com / Illuminate2026! and client client@example.com / client123 both authenticated successfully against /api/auth/login (cookies set).
seed_data_creation: Tests created (and intentionally did not clean up to allow chain reuse): a TEST_user_<uuid>@example.com registered user (logged out at test end), a TEST_Portfolio item, a TEST_booking for the client, a TEST_Contract document (signed), a TEST_Invoice ($50 AUD unpaid), a TEST_Gallery with one 1x1 PNG photo, and one Luma session. Prefix 'TEST_' was used consistently for easy admin cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. The Luma multi-turn test is marked xfail because the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in this env has a $0.001 budget cap — first turn passes and proves the contract (session_id returned, get_active_services tool called). The payments status 500 is reproducible: Emergent Stripe proxy returns 404 on the freshly minted session_id; the fix should at minimum be defensive error handling in payments_routes.status. After main agent fixes those two routes, re-run the full file; expect 16/16 once LLM budget allows the full booking confirmation turn.

rca_of_the_issue.stripe_status_500: Reproduction: 1) login as client@example.com; 2) POST /api/payments/checkout/invoice with a valid invoice_id and any https origin_url -> 200 with session_id (Emergent Stripe POST returns 200); 3) immediately GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} -> 500. Backend logs: stripe error_code=resource_missing 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' followed by emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError uncaught. Mitigation: wrap stripe.get_checkout_status in try/except and report 502 + detail; longer-term escalate to Emergent Stripe team.
rca_of_the_issue.luma_multi_turn_500: Reproduction: send any second message reusing the session_id from a working first turn -> 500. Backend logs: 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.001164, Max budget: 0.001' from the litellm/openai proxy. Root cause: tiny budget on EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in this environment + no exception handling in luma_routes.chat. Mitigation: try/except around chat_step returning graceful fallback + request larger budget.
